auth.sign_in_subtitle: Sign in to manage your merchant account.
auth.forgot_password_hint: We'll email you a secure link to reset your password.
auth.session_expired: Your session expired. Please sign in again.
auth.generic_error: We could not sign you in. Please try again.
auth.rate_limited: Too many login attempts. Please wait a minute and try again.
auth.hero_title: Everything your merchant needs, in one portal.
auth.hero_subtitle: Configure branches, manage your POS staff, build your menu, and watch your sales — all from a workspace built for Oman.

change_password.title: Change password
change_password.subtitle: Update the password you use to sign in.
change_password.subtitle_forced: For your security, choose your own password before continuing.
change_password.mismatch: The new passwords do not match.
change_password.error_generic: Could not update your password. Please try again.

forgot.title: Forgot your password?
forgot.subtitle: Enter the email you sign in with and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
forgot.success_body: If an account exists for {email}, a password reset link is on its way. The link expires in 60 minutes.
forgot.error_generic: Could not send the reset link. Please try again.

reset.title: Choose a new password
reset.subtitle: Set a new password for {email}.
reset.success_body: Your password has been updated. Sign in with your new password.
reset.invalid_body: This reset link is no longer valid. Reset links expire after 60 minutes and can be used once — request a fresh one below.
reset.error_generic: Could not reset your password. Please try again.

two_factor.title: Two-step verification
two_factor.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to finish signing in.
two_factor.subtitle_recovery: Enter one of your one-time recovery codes to finish signing in.
two_factor.invalid_code: The provided two-factor code is invalid.
two_factor.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
two_factor.expired_body: Your sign-in attempt timed out for security. Please sign in again with your email and password.

profile.title: My Profile
profile.subtitle: Your account details for the merchant portal.
profile.name: Display name
profile.email_note: Your email is your sign-in identifier and is managed by your MITHQAL admin — it cannot be changed here.
profile.change_password_note: Update the password you use to sign in.
profile.error_generic: Could not update your profile. Please try again.

two_factor.title: Two-factor authentication
two_factor.subtitle: Add a second step to your sign-in with an authenticator app.
two_factor.disabled_body: Protect your account with time-based one-time codes (TOTP). After your password, you will be asked for a 6-digit code from an app like Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, or 1Password.
two_factor.scan_hint: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app, or enter the secret manually. Then type the 6-digit code the app shows to confirm.
two_factor.enabled_notice: Two-factor authentication is now enabled for your account.
two_factor.recovery_note: Store these one-time codes somewhere safe (a password manager). Each works once if you lose access to your authenticator app. They will NOT be shown again.
two_factor.enabled_body: Your account asks for a 6-digit authenticator code at every sign-in. Disabling removes that protection.
two_factor.disable_note: To disable, confirm your password plus a current authenticator code (or one unused recovery code).
two_factor.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

purchase_receipts.title: Purchase Receipts
purchase_receipts.subtitle: Goods Received Notes — record a whole delivery in one document.
purchase_receipts.empty_state: No purchase receipts yet. Record your first delivery.

form.title: Goods Received Note
form.subtitle: Add every item in the delivery, its cost, and where it goes.
form.load_failed: Could not load the form data.
form.distribute_hint: Split this line across branches. Whatever you leave stays in the central warehouse.
form.remainder: Distributing {distributed} of {total} — {remainder} stays in the warehouse
form.needs_quantity: Enter a quantity for this item, or remove the row.
form.charges_hint: Named receipt-level costs (delivery, customs…). Each books its own expense.

payment.title: Payment
payment.credit_hint: The cost still counts now; this only tracks what you owe the supplier.
payment.amount_required: Enter an amount greater than zero.

dashboard.title: Welcome
dashboard.subtitle: This is your merchant control panel. As MITHQAL provisions more capabilities, they will appear here.

portal_users.title: Portal Users
portal_users.subtitle: Invite teammates into your MITHQAL workspace, decide which branches they can see, and rotate credentials when needed.
portal_users.empty_state: No teammates yet. Create your first one to get started.
portal_users.no_branches: No branches yet — create a branch first to restrict access.

assign_roles.title: Assign roles
assign_roles.hint: Pick one or more roles. The user gets the union of every assigned role's permissions.
assign_roles.no_roles: No roles available yet. Create one in Roles & Permissions first.

create.title: Create a new teammate
create.subtitle: We will generate a one-time password you can share with them out of band.

password_modal.title: One-time password
password_modal.subtitle: Copy this password and share it with {name} ({email}) through a secure channel.
password_modal.one_shot_warning: This password is shown once. After you close this dialog it cannot be retrieved — only reset.

pos_staff.title: POS Staff
pos_staff.subtitle: Hire the cashiers, waiters, kitchen, and supervisors who run your registers. Each one gets a 6-digit PIN they use to unlock the POS device.
pos_staff.search_placeholder: Search by name, staff code, or branch…
pos_staff.empty_state: No staff yet. Hire your first teammate to get started.
pos_staff.no_branches_hint: Add at least one branch before hiring staff — every staff member belongs to a branch.

create.title: Hire a new staff member
create.subtitle: We will generate a 6-digit PIN you can share with them.

pin_modal.title: One-time PIN
pin_modal.subtitle: Share this PIN with {name} through a secure channel. They will type it into the POS device to unlock their shift.
pin_modal.one_shot_warning: This PIN is shown once. After you close this dialog it cannot be retrieved — only reset.

terminate_dialog.title: Terminate employment?
terminate_dialog.body: {name} will be removed from the roster and their PIN will stop working immediately.
terminate_dialog.note: The record stays in your audit trail so past order history still attributes correctly.

branches.title: Branches
branches.subtitle: Rename your locations, fix manager contact details, set opening hours, and tune POS defaults for each branch.
branches.search_placeholder: Search by name, code, or manager…
branches.empty_state: No branches yet. Contact your MITHQAL account manager to onboard a new location.
branches.add_branch_hint: Need a new branch? Adding or removing a branch is an admin operation — file a support ticket and your MITHQAL account manager will provision it for you.
branches.permission_error: You do not have permission to perform that action.

fields.name: Branch name
fields.status_disabled_hint: Only an account super admin can activate or deactivate a branch.

devices.title: Devices on this branch
devices.read_only_hint: Devices are assigned and managed by the admin. You can see them here but cannot change them.
devices.empty: No devices assigned to this branch yet.

show.sales_by_hour_sub: Last {days} days · day of week × hour
show.no_products: No products carried at this branch yet.
show.no_staff: No staff assigned to this branch.
show.receipt_template_hint: Customize what this branch's POS prints

add_stock.title: Add stock to {branch}
add_stock.no_products: This branch has no ready/bought-in products to top up.
add_stock.unit_cost_hint: Leave blank to use the ingredient's default cost.

assign_staff.title: Assign staff to {branch}
assign_staff.name: Name
assign_staff.branch_note: This staff member will be hired into {branch}.
assign_staff.pin_hint: Write this down — it is shown only once.
assign_staff.failed: Could not assign the staff member.

receipt.title: Branch receipt template
receipt.intro: Set what this branch's POS device prints on each receipt. Leave a field blank to omit it; an empty template falls back to the default receipt.
receipt.logo_hint: PNG/JPG; resized to 384px and printed in greyscale.
receipt.logo_invalid: That file isn't a valid image.
receipt.logo_too_large: That logo is too large — try a smaller or simpler image.
receipt.save_failed: Could not save the receipt template.

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.subtitle: Define which features each role can access, then assign those roles to your portal users.
roles.system_hint: System roles are part of MITHQAL's defaults. You can edit which permissions they hold but can't rename or delete them.
roles.delete_blocked_hint: Re-assign the users holding this role before deleting it.

editor.system_role_hint: This is a system role — the name cannot be changed but you can edit which permissions it holds.

delete_dialog.title: Delete role?
delete_dialog.body: The role "{name}" will be removed. This cannot be undone.

floor_plan.title: Floor Plan
floor_plan.subtitle: Define the floors at each branch and the tables on each floor. Tables get a unique QR code customers can scan to open the menu on their phone.
floor_plan.no_branches: Add a branch first to build a floor plan.
floor_plan.empty_state: No floors yet for this branch.
floor_plan.empty_hint: Add your first floor (Main Hall, Patio, VIP, etc.) to get started.
floor_plan.no_tables: No tables yet on this floor. Add one to get started.
floor_plan.delete_floor_blocked: Move or delete this floor's tables before deleting the floor.

planner.title: Planner — {name}
planner.confirm_discard: You have unsaved layout changes. Discard?
planner.help_can_manage: Drag tables to position them. Snaps to a 20px grid. Press Esc to cancel a drag in progress.
planner.help_view_only: Read-only view. Only users with floor-plan manage permission can move tables.
planner.no_tables_hint: Add a table to this floor before opening the planner.

qr_modal.title: QR code for {label}
qr_modal.subtitle: Customers scan this to open your menu on their phone. Print the token onto a card and place it at the table.
qr_modal.menu_url_hint: Menu URL will be /menu?t={token} once the customer-facing app ships.

delete_table_dialog.title: Delete table?
delete_table_dialog.body: Delete table "{label}"? Past orders that reference it will still resolve.

wizard.forbidden: You need the catalogue manage permission to add or edit products.
wizard.not_found: This product doesn't exist (or was deleted).
wizard.save_failed: Saving failed. Nothing was changed — try again.
wizard.name_required: Give the product a name before continuing.
wizard.price_required: Set a base price (0 is allowed) before continuing.
wizard.type_hint: Pick this first — it decides which sections the rest of the form shows.

ingredient.label: Made to order
ingredient.desc: The recipe is consumed at sale; availability comes from branch ingredient stock.

cooked.label: Cooked
cooked.desc: The kitchen produces batches ahead of sale (recipe consumed at production); pieces sell from shelf stock and start sold out until produced.

unit.label: Ready / bought-in
unit.desc: Finished goods counted by piece — manage counts from the Stock button on the product row.

wizard.shelf_life_hint: Default expiry for each kitchen batch (the chef can adjust per batch at Finish). Leave empty if it keeps — no day-end disposition will be asked.
wizard.owned_hint: Add-on groups that belong to THIS product only (e.g. its sizes). Build them right here — they are created together with the product.
wizard.physical_hint: Countable goods consumed when one unit sells — coffee = 1 × cup 12oz + 1 × lid. Deducted from the branch's unit stock at sale.
wizard.physical_empty: No physical items yet. Create them under Inventory → Physical items (e.g. "Cup 12oz", kind: used with food) and they appear here.
wizard.review_hint: Check everything below, then confirm. Nothing is saved until you do.
wizard.review_hint_edit: Check everything below, then confirm. Field, recipe, item and branch changes apply when you confirm — add-on group changes made on step 2 are already live.
wizard.owned_hint_edit: Add-on groups that belong to THIS product only (e.g. its sizes). Changes here apply immediately — they don't wait for the confirm step.
wizard.unsaved_confirm: You have unsaved changes — leave this page and discard them?
wizard.branches_none_selected: No branches ticked — this saves as available at EVERY branch (and clears any per-branch rows).
wizard.branches_recipe_hint: A made-to-order product has no piece count — you only choose WHERE it's offered. Whether a branch can make it follows that branch's ingredient stock (and any physical items).
wizard.branches_cooked_hint: A cooked product has no manual units — you only choose WHERE it's offered. Its shelf count at each branch comes from kitchen production and starts sold out until the kitchen produces.
wizard.recipe_dropped: This product type doesn't carry a recipe — the recipe lines you entered will not be saved.

catalogue.title: Catalogue
catalogue.subtitle: Manage menu categories, products, and add-on options. Recipes and inventory mapping arrive in the next phase.
catalogue.delete_category_blocked: Move or delete the category's products before deleting it.
catalogue.empty_categories: No categories yet. Add one to start building your menu.
catalogue.empty_products: No products yet. Add one to start your menu.
catalogue.empty_addon_groups: No add-on groups yet. Add one to let cashiers attach modifiers like milk choice, sugar level, or extras.

fields.name: Name
fields.delivery_price_hint: Leave blank to use the base price. Set when delivery orders need a higher price (to absorb aggregator commissions, etc.).
fields.tax_rate_hint: Leave blank to inherit the company default (5%).
fields.tax_inclusive_hint: Display label only for now — order totals still add company taxes on top.
fields.show_on_tablet_hint: Controls the future customer-facing tablet menu. The staff POS is unaffected.
fields.low_stock_threshold_hint: Show a LOW STOCK badge on the POS card when branch units fall to or below this. Leave blank for no badge.
fields.available_hours_hint: POS devices only sell this product during this daily window. Leave both empty for always available. An end before the start wraps past midnight (e.g. 22:00 to 02:00).
fields.is_global_hint: Global groups attach automatically to every product. Otherwise pick which products use this group from each product's edit screen.
fields.min_selections_hint: 1 or more makes this group required — the POS blocks add-to-cart until satisfied.
fields.bound_categories_hint: The group applies to every product in the ticked categories (in addition to per-product attachments).
fields.is_default_hint: This option starts selected in the POS customize sheet. A single-choice group can only have one default.
fields.addon_groups_hint: Pick which add-on groups appear when this product is selected on the POS. Global groups are always included and not shown here.

consumption.section_hint: What picking this option uses from stock — or removes from the product's own usage (e.g. "no salad"). Removals never push consumption below zero at sale time.
consumption.new_option_title: Stock usage for the new option (optional)

catalogue.delete_addon_group_blocked: Detach this add-on group from all products before deleting it.

delete_addon_group_dialog.title: Delete add-on group?
delete_addon_group_dialog.body: Delete "{name}" and all its options? This cannot be undone.

delete_addon_dialog.title: Delete option?
delete_addon_dialog.body: Delete "{name}"? Past orders that reference it will still resolve via the soft-delete trail.

product_addons.title: Add-ons unique to this product
product_addons.hint: Options only this product offers. Shared add-on groups are managed in the Add-ons tab.
product_addons.save_first: Save the product first to add its own add-ons.

recipe.section_hint: Map ingredients consumed per sale. A latte might use 0.180 L milk + 0.018 kg espresso beans. Empty recipe = pre-made goods, no inventory deduction on sale.
recipe.no_lines: No recipe yet — this product is treated as pre-made.
recipe.no_ingredients_hint: Create an ingredient on the Inventory page first, then come back to build the recipe.
recipe.live_cost_hint: Sum of (quantity × ingredient default cost) at current prices. Phase 8 sale orders snapshot the historical cost separately.
recipe.margin_hint: Computed as (base price − recipe cost) ÷ base price.
recipe.duplicate_ingredient: Each ingredient can appear at most once in the recipe.

delete_prod_dialog.title: Delete product?
delete_prod_dialog.body: Delete "{name}"? Past orders that reference it will still resolve via the soft-delete trail.

branches.section_hint: Choose which branches sell this product and set per-branch units. Branch location & devices are managed by the admin.
branches.all_branches_hint: This product shows on every branch POS. Untick to choose specific branches.

cat_branches.section_hint: Choose which branch menus show this category. Devices at other branches hide it from their category strip.
cat_branches.all_branches_hint: This category shows on every branch POS. Untick to choose specific branches.

production.title: Kitchen Production
production.subtitle: Every cooked-product batch the kitchen ran: who made it, what it consumed, and how long it took. Batches are recorded by the POS device — this page is read-only.
production.empty_state: No production batches yet. They appear here once the kitchen starts producing on the POS device.

detail.no_lines: No ingredient lines (product has no recipe).

branch_targets.title: Branch Targets
branch_targets.subtitle: Sales goals per branch — an amount per day, week or month, evaluated cumulatively over back-to-back windows.
branch_targets.delete_confirm: Delete this target? Its window history is kept, but no new windows will be evaluated.
branch_targets.create_hint: Creating a target replaces the branch's current active one (its history is kept). The period, window size and start date are fixed after creation — to change them, create a replacement.
branch_targets.edit_hint: Only the goal amount and the active flag can change. The new amount applies to the current and future windows; finished windows keep the goal they were evaluated against.
branch_targets.empty: No targets yet — set the first branch goal.

dashboard_targets.title: Branch Performance
dashboard_targets.miss_popup_body: These branches finished their last evaluation window below the goal:

deliveries.title: Deliveries
deliveries.subtitle: Reconcile delivery-provider statements — confirm matched orders or adjust to what was actually received.
deliveries.adjusted_ok: Order settled at the received amount.
deliveries.adjust_hint: The provider paid a different amount than expected. Enter what was actually received — the difference is stored as a reconciliation variance and the order settles at this amount.

device_live.title: Live device — {name}
device_live.not_enrolled: This device has no MDM kiosk id yet — ask the platform admin to enroll it in scalefusion.
device_live.confirm_message: This sends "{action}" to the device immediately. Continue?
device_live.message_hint: Shows on the device at the OS level (even outside the POS app) via the MDM agent.

messages.title: Messages
messages.subtitle: Your internal inbox, plus announcements to the POS devices with read receipts.
messages.announce_hint: Shows on the POS with a popup and an unread badge. A staff-targeted message appears only when that person is logged in.
messages.retracted_ok: Announcement retracted — devices will remove it.
messages.retract_confirm: Retract this announcement? Every device will remove it and this cannot be undone.
messages.empty_announcements: No announcements yet. Send one — it reaches the POS devices live.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.subtitle: Manage ingredients, suppliers, and per-branch stock balances. Every change writes to an append-only movement ledger.
inventory.no_branches: Add a branch first to track stock.

physical_items.subtitle: Things that cannot be eaten — cups, boxes, wrappers (used with food) and bulbs, wires, cleaning items (branch use). Fully stock-managed; receiving with a cost books a Stock purchases expense.
physical_items.purpose_packaging_hint: Cups, lids, boxes, wrappers… Attachable to products and counted per sale.
physical_items.purpose_general_hint: Light bulbs, wires, cleaning items, devices… Branch inventory only — never attached to food.
physical_items.cost_hint: Used to suggest the purchase total when you receive stock.
physical_items.delete_body: Delete "{name}"? Its stock history is kept; the item disappears from every list and picker. Items still attached to a product's composition must be detached first.

fields.name: Name
fields.min_stock_threshold_hint: Below this quantity the stock is flagged as Low. Leave blank to never flag.
fields.signed_quantity_hint: Positive = found more than expected, negative = found less.
fields.note_required_hint: Explain why the stock count changed. This shows in the audit log.
fields.unit_cost_hint: Leave blank to use the ingredient's default cost.

inventory.empty_ingredients: No ingredients yet. Add one to start tracking stock.
inventory.empty_suppliers: No suppliers yet. Add one so ingredients can reference them.
inventory.empty_stock: No stock recorded at this branch yet. Use Restock above to log your opening inventory.
inventory.empty_movements: No movements at this branch yet.
inventory.delete_ingredient_blocked: Adjust stock to zero in every branch before deleting this ingredient.
inventory.delete_supplier_blocked: Re-assign or clear ingredients that name this supplier before deleting.

purchase_cost.hint: What you paid for this stock — booked automatically as an expense. Delivery is booked as a separate expense.
purchase_cost.no_cost_hint: No expense will be booked for this receive.

alt_units.title: Alternate units
alt_units.hint: Optional larger or packaged units. The factor is how many base units make up one of this unit (e.g. base g, alt kg, factor 1000).
alt_units.auto_provided: Also accepts {units} automatically — no need to add them.
alt_units.name: Unit name
alt_units.factor_hint: Base units per 1 of this unit. Must be greater than 0.
alt_units.save_first_hint: Save the ingredient first to add alternate units.
alt_units.name_immutable_hint: The unit name can't be changed after it's created.

errors.save_failed: Could not save the alternate unit.
errors.delete_failed: Could not delete the alternate unit.

adjust_modal.title: Adjust stock — {ingredient}
adjust_modal.subtitle: Records an Adjustment movement. Use a positive quantity if a physical count shows more than the system says; use negative if it shows less.

restock_modal.title: Restock — {ingredient}
restock_modal.subtitle: Logs a positive inflow at the chosen unit cost. Optional supplier reference helps the future supplier-performance report.

delete_ing_dialog.title: Delete ingredient?
delete_ing_dialog.body: Delete "{name}"? This cannot be undone. Historical stock movements will still resolve via the soft-delete trail.

delete_sup_dialog.title: Delete supplier?
delete_sup_dialog.body: Delete "{name}"? Historical movements that referenced this supplier will show "no longer on file" instead.

waste.empty: No waste recorded yet. Use Record waste to log the first event.
waste.no_branch: Pick a branch above to see its waste history.

modal.title: Record waste at {branch}
modal.notes_help_other: Notes are required when reason is Other.
modal.occurred_at_help: Leave blank to record as now.
modal.cost_preview_hint: Quantity × current unit cost — server freezes this at record time.
modal.insufficient_warning: Not enough stock at this branch to absorb this waste — adjust first.

piece.title: Piece unit (physical counting)
piece.hint: Set these when staff physically handle whole pieces — bottles, crates, bags. Purchases and day-end counts are then entered in pieces and converted automatically.
piece.label: Piece label
piece.allow_fractional_hint: Untick for items like eggs where 4.7 pieces makes no sense — counts and purchases must then be whole numbers.

purchase_modal.title: Record a purchase
purchase_modal.subtitle: Enter what was physically received and what was paid — the unit cost is computed, never typed. Entering pieces AND total quantity marks a loose batch and updates the piece ratio.
purchase_modal.units_hint: Required for loose produce (weigh the batch); optional when the piece ratio is fixed.
purchase_modal.success: Purchase recorded - stock, cost and ratio updated.

counts.empty_hint: Run a day-end count to reconcile what is physically on the shelf against the books.
counts.success_clean: Count submitted - all {lines} lines matched the books exactly.
counts.success_with_variance: Count submitted - {variance} of {lines} lines had a variance; movements written.

modal.title: Day-end count at {branch}
modal.subtitle: Count what is physically on the shelf. Piece-tracked ingredients are counted in pieces; leave a row blank to skip it. Shortfalls are written as waste (count variance), overages as adjustments.
modal.no_stock: No stock has ever been recorded at this branch.
modal.empty_error: Enter at least one counted amount.

restock.empty: No restock requests yet. Branches can submit one from the Branch Stock tab — or create a draft here.

create_modal.duplicate_warning: An ingredient appears more than once — merge them into a single line.

review_modal.reject_hint: A rejection reason is required — the requester needs to know why.

cancel_modal.title: Cancel request
cancel_modal.hint: Optional reason (stored on the request for audit).

allocate_modal.title: Allocate request
allocate_modal.hint: Default = send the full requested amount of every line. Override per line for partial fulfillment, or set 0 to skip a line.

transfers.empty: No transfers yet. Move ingredients between branches with the New transfer button.

create_modal.title: New branch transfer
create_modal.same_branch: Source and destination must be different branches.
create_modal.no_lines: Add at least one ingredient line.
create_modal.note_placeholder: e.g. covering a shortfall at the other branch
create_modal.duplicate_warning: An ingredient appears more than once — merge them into a single line.

restock_suggestions.title: Smart restock suggestions
restock_suggestions.subtitle: Forecast for {branch}, based on recent consumption. Tick the items to reorder, tweak quantities, then create a restock request.
restock_suggestions.window_days_hint: How many past days of consumption to average. 1–365.
restock_suggestions.cover_days_hint: Days of demand the suggested quantity should cover. 1–365.
restock_suggestions.empty: Nothing to reorder — stock is healthy for the chosen window.
restock_suggestions.note_placeholder: e.g. ahead of the weekend rush
restock_suggestions.read_only_note: You can review these suggestions, but creating a restock request needs the restock-request permission.
restock_suggestions.no_selection: Tick at least one item with a quantity to create a request.
restock_suggestions.created: Restock request created with {count} item. | Restock request created with {count} items.
restock_suggestions.create_failed: Failed to create the restock request.

customers.title: Customers
customers.subtitle: Your customer book — lookup by name, phone, or vehicle plate. Attach multiple plates per customer for drive-thru recognition.
customers.search_placeholder: Search by name, phone, or plate…
customers.empty_state: No customers yet. Add your first one to get started.

delete.title: Delete customer?
delete.confirm: Remove {name} from your customer book? Vehicle plates remain attached and the customer can be restored from the audit log.

errors.duplicate_plate_local: That plate is already in your pending list.
errors.duplicate_tag_local: That tag is already in the list.
errors.save_failed: Could not save the customer. Try again.

loyalty.title: Loyalty Rules
loyalty.subtitle: Define visit-based stamp cards and spend-based point rules. Multiple can run in parallel; pause or resume any rule without deleting it. Per-customer balances live on the Customers page.
loyalty.empty: No loyalty rules yet. Create a points or stamp-card rule to get started.

modal.type_locked: Type can't change after creation — create a new rule instead.

delete.title: Delete loyalty rule?
delete.confirm: Delete {name}? Customer balances under this rule are retained for history; the rule disappears from the POS picker.

customer.title: Loyalty & wallet
customer.wallet_hint: Top up takes a positive amount with optional reason. Adjust takes a signed amount + required reason.

errors.rule_save_failed: Could not save the loyalty rule.

taxes.title: Taxes
taxes.subtitle: Define the taxes your company collects (e.g. VAT, Municipality). The POS fetches the active set when staff log in and adds each, as its own line, on top of every order total.

recoverable.title: Purchase tax is recoverable
recoverable.hint: When on, the tax you record on purchases (on the Goods Received Note, stock receives and expenses) is credited back into net profit on the Sales report — like a reclaimable VAT receivable. When off, it's just tracked as a figure.

taxes.empty_state: No taxes yet. Add VAT or any charge your company collects.
taxes.delete_confirm: Delete {name}? It stops applying at the POS on the next sync.

expense_categories.title: Expense categories
expense_categories.subtitle: Define the categories your team picks when logging expenses. The POS fetches the active set; existing expenses keep their category even if you hide it.
expense_categories.empty_state: No categories yet. Add your first one.
expense_categories.delete_confirm: Delete {name}? It stops being offered at the POS; past expenses keep it.

delivery_providers.subtitle: Manage the third-party aggregators your branches deliver through. Per-product prices live on each product (see the Provider Pricing section in the product modal).
delivery_providers.empty_state: No providers yet. Add Talabat, Otlob, or any aggregator you use.

fields.name: Provider name
fields.color_hint: Used by the POS picker to distinguish providers at a glance.
fields.commission_hint: The provider's cut. Expected payout per order = punched total minus this percentage (frozen onto each order at punch time).

delete.title: Delete provider?
delete.confirm: Soft-delete {name}? Per-product price overrides stay in place for historical orders, and the provider can be restored from the audit log.

product_grid.title: Provider pricing
product_grid.hint: Set a per-provider override. Empty = fall back to the product's delivery price, then to the base price.

errors.prices_load_failed: Could not load this product's provider prices.

discounts.title: Discounts
discounts.subtitle: Configure promo rules that the POS applies automatically. Combine percent + fixed amounts across products, categories, and order totals.
discounts.empty_state: No discount rules yet. Add a Ramadan promo or a happy-hour rule to get started.

fields.name: Name
fields.target_products_hint: Select one or more products. The rule will only fire on lines whose product is checked here.
fields.time_hint: Leave blank for all day. Crossing midnight is supported (e.g. 22:00 to 02:00).
fields.stackable: Stackable (can combine with other discounts + loyalty)
fields.auto_apply_hint: When on, the POS applies this discount by itself to every qualifying order. When off, the cashier picks it from the discounts list.
fields.auto_apply_targeted_hint: Product and category discounts always apply automatically to matching items.

delete.title: Delete discount?
delete.confirm: Soft-delete {name}? Historical orders that used this rule keep the snapshotted amount; the rule disappears from the POS picker.

offers.title: Offers
offers.subtitle: Build promotions the POS applies on its own — buy-one-get-one, fixed-price bundles, multi-buy deals, cheapest-item-free, and spend thresholds.
offers.empty_state: No offers yet. Add a meal deal or a buy-one-get-one to get started.

type_hints.bogo: Buy a qualifying quantity, get items free or at a percent off.
type_hints.bundle: A fixed-price set the cashier composes from pick groups (e.g. main + drink).
type_hints.multi_buy: A quantity of qualifying items for one fixed price (e.g. 3 for 1.000 OMR).
type_hints.cheapest_free: Buy a quantity of qualifying items; the cheapest one(s) are free.
type_hints.spend_get: When the order subtotal passes a threshold, grant a reward.

fields.name: Name
fields.time_hint: Leave blank for all day. Crossing midnight is supported (e.g. 22:00 to 02:00).
fields.auto_apply_hint: When on, the POS applies this offer by itself to every qualifying order. When off, the cashier picks it.
fields.bundle_cashier_hint: Bundles are picked by the cashier — they are never applied automatically.

bogo.same_as_buy: Same items as the buy list
bogo.percent_hint: 100% = free. Lower values discount the eligible items instead.

cheapest_free.hint: Free items must be fewer than the buy quantity.

delete.title: Delete offer?
delete.confirm: Soft-delete {name}? Historical orders that used this offer keep the snapshotted amounts; the offer disappears from the POS.

reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Tenant reports across sales, customers, inventory, staff, and more. Filter by date window and (optionally) branch.

sales.title: Sales
sales.desc: Gross + net + discounts + tax + refunds by hour, weekday, payment, type, branch.

customers.title: Customers
customers.desc: Top customers, new vs returning, loyalty points issued + redeemed + liability.

discounts.title: Discounts
discounts.desc: Total discount value, share of gross, by branch (rule + staff land in Phase 8).

discounted_comped_products.title: Discounted & comped products
discounted_comped_products.desc: Exactly which product was discounted or comped, by type (offer, discount, comp, gift), how many, and the money off.

comps.title: Comps
comps.desc: Manager write-offs: total comp value, by reason, by approving manager, recent comps.

shifts.title: Shifts
shifts.desc: Cash variance per shift: float, expected vs counted, short drawers, same-day re-open.

payouts.title: Payouts
payouts.desc: Commission breakdown: your net take-home, platform fee, bank fee, by branch.

product_performance.title: Product Performance
product_performance.desc: Top sellers by revenue + qty, slow movers, top add-ons.

recipe_cost.title: Recipe & Cost
recipe_cost.desc: Per-product theoretical cost, profit, margin (trend lands in Phase 8).

staff_activity.title: Staff Activity
staff_activity.desc: Per-staff orders paid, revenue, avg ticket, voids, discounts, hours logged.

inventory_consumption.title: Inventory Consumption
inventory_consumption.desc: Per-ingredient consumed in window, days of stock, low-stock flag.

loss_waste.title: Loss / Waste
loss_waste.desc: Total waste value, by branch, by reason, top wasted ingredients.

restock_purchasing.title: Restock / Purchasing
restock_purchasing.desc: Restock spend by supplier, by branch, top purchased ingredients.

round_up_donation.title: Round-Up Donations
round_up_donation.desc: Total raised, donations by status, by branch.

discounted_comped_products.types_hint: Every price reduction, by the exact product. Loyalty redemptions are counted under "Discount" (they aren't separable from other discounts in the data).
discounted_comped_products.whole_order_hint: Applied to the whole order, not a single product.

shortfall.title: Portion-control variance
shortfall.subtitle: What sales recipes should have used vs what actually left stock. A persistent overage means over-portioning, waste, or theft.

product_dispositions.title: Day-end product dispositions
product_dispositions.subtitle: Cooked / piece-counted products wasted or given away at day end. Value is cost-based (product cost price), not retail.

expenses.title: Expenses
expenses.subtitle: Review expenses captured from the POS (and logged here in the back office). Approve, reject with a reason, or annotate. Reviewed + recorded expenses feed the net-profit line of the Sales Report.
expenses.empty: No expenses match the current filters.

dashboard_widgets.low_stock_subtitle: Ingredients with a balance below their minimum threshold across all branches.
dashboard_widgets.load_failed: Could not load the dashboard summary.
dashboard_widgets.devices_online: Online now, of {total} devices in your fleet.
dashboard_widgets.sales_by_hour_sub: Paid sales by day & hour, last {days} days

audit_log.title: Audit Log
audit_log.subtitle: Every audit-able action by your team, with actor, target, and before/after snapshots. Restricted to roles with the audit_log.view permission.
audit_log.no_rows: No audit events match the current filters.
audit_log.load_failed: Could not load the audit log.

orders.title: Sales / Orders
orders.subtitle: Every order across your branches. Filter by date, branch, or status.

detail.title: Order detail
detail.no_loyalty: No loyalty activity on this order.

pos.title: POS Settings
pos.subtitle: Configure POS terminal policies — order cancellation, manager approval, device reports and kitchen access, and void/comp reasons.

order_cancellation.title: Order Cancellation
order_cancellation.subtitle: Choose which staff positions may cancel an order at the POS. Only the selected positions can void or cancel orders on the terminal.
order_cancellation.forbidden: You don't have permission to manage this setting.
order_cancellation.save_failed: Could not save the order cancellation policy.

manager_approval.title: Manager Approval
manager_approval.subtitle: Choose which staff positions can approve sensitive POS actions (comps, cancellations, gifts) by entering their PIN when a manager fingerprint is unavailable.
manager_approval.save_failed: Could not save the manager approval policy.

reports_positions.title: Device reports access
reports_positions.subtitle: Choose which staff positions can open the Reports dashboard on the POS device. Staff in other positions won't see branch sales figures on the terminal.
reports_positions.positions_label: Positions allowed to view device reports
reports_positions.save_failed: Could not save the device reports access policy.

kitchen_positions.title: Device kitchen access
kitchen_positions.subtitle: Kitchen staff always have access. Choose any OTHER positions that should also be able to open the Kitchen production screen on the POS device.
kitchen_positions.positions_label: Other positions allowed to use the device kitchen
kitchen_positions.always_hint: The kitchen role always has access. Tick other roles to grant them kitchen access too. Leave all unticked to keep it kitchen-only — to give a specific person access, set their position to Kitchen on the POS Staff page.
kitchen_positions.save_failed: Could not save the device kitchen access policy.

order_numbering.title: Order Numbering
order_numbering.subtitle: Define how POS order numbers look on receipts: a prefix plus a zero-padded counter (e.g. KLD-0042). The server hands out the numbers, so every device at the same branch or company shares one sequence.
order_numbering.enable_hint: When off, receipts show no custom number. Offline devices fall back to a local counter until they reconnect.
order_numbering.prefix_hint: Up to 8 characters, printed before the counter. Leave empty for digits only.
order_numbering.scope_branch_hint: Each branch counts its own orders independently (Branch A and Branch B can both have a No. 0042).
order_numbering.scope_company_hint: One shared sequence across the whole company — every order number is unique company-wide.
order_numbering.daily_reset_hint: The counter goes back to 1 each morning — common for call-out numbers shouted to customers.
order_numbering.forbidden: You don't have permission to manage this setting.
order_numbering.save_failed: Could not save the order numbering setting.

reasons.void_subtitle: Every order void at the POS must pick one of these. "Food was made" keeps the ingredients consumed and shows the loss in the Loss/Waste report.
reasons.comp_subtitle: Manager-approved write-offs (free items, service recovery). Inventory deducts as if sold; the value is reported separately from discounts.
reasons.name: Name
reasons.affects_inventory_hint: Tick when the items were actually prepared (Quality Issue) — voiding then keeps the ingredients consumed. Untick when nothing was made (Wrong Order Entry) — stock is restored.
reasons.max_amount_hint: The largest single comp allowed under this reason. Leave blank for no cap.
reasons.delete_confirm: Delete "{name}"? Historical orders keep their snapshot of it.
